---
title: "Lesson 1: Disaster Plan"
slug: ch5-exercise1-disaster-plan-vocabulary
format:
    html:
        toc: true
        html-math-method: katex
---

## Practice Exercises

### 1. Complete the words based on the meanings provided:

```{=html}
<table>
  <tr>
    <th>Meaning</th>
    <th>Complete the word</th>
    <th>Feedback</th>
  </tr>

  <tr>
    <td>A violent storm with strong wind and rain</td>
    <td><input id="a1" placeholder="Type the word"></td>
    <td id="f1"></td>
  </tr>

  <tr>
    <td>A sudden shaking of the ground</td>
    <td><input id="a2" placeholder="Type the word"></td>
    <td id="f2"></td>
  </tr>

  <tr>
    <td>Water covering land that is usually dry</td>
    <td><input id="a3" placeholder="Type the word"></td>
    <td id="f3"></td>
  </tr>

  <tr>
    <td>A huge ocean wave caused by an underwater earthquake</td>
    <td><input id="a4" placeholder="Type the word"></td>
    <td id="f4"></td>
  </tr>

  <tr>
    <td>A message that tells people about danger</td>
    <td><input id="a5" placeholder="Type the word"></td>
    <td id="f5"></td>
  </tr>
</table>
<button onclick="checkAnswers()">Check Answers</button>

<script>
  function checkAnswers() {
    const answers = [
      "hurricane",
      "earthquake",
      "flood",
      "tsunami",
      "warning"
    ];

    answers.forEach((correct, i) => {
      const input = document.getElementById("a" + (i + 1)).value
        .toLowerCase()
        .trim();
      const feedback = document.getElementById("f" + (i + 1));

      if (input === correct) {
        feedback.innerHTML = "✔ Correct";
        feedback.className = "correct";
      } else {
        feedback.innerHTML = "✘ Wrong";
        feedback.className = "wrong";
      }
    });
  }
</script>
```

### 2. Fill in the blanks in the sentences using the vocabulary words:

```{=html}
<div class="question">
  1. The strong winds and rain from the 
  <input id="q1" placeholder="type answer"> 
  damaged many houses.
  <span id="fb1"></span>
</div>

<div class="question">
  2. The ground began to 
  <input id="q2" placeholder="type answer"> 
  during the earthquake.
  <span id="fb2"></span>
</div>

<div class="question">
  3. Heavy rain caused a 
  <input id="q3" placeholder="type answer"> 
  in the village.
  <span id="fb3"></span>
</div>

<div class="question">
  4. The loud 
  <input id="q4" placeholder="type answer"> 
  helped people move to a safe place.
  <span id="fb4"></span>
</div>

<div class="question">
  5. Parents try to 
  <input id="q5" placeholder="type answer"> 
  their children during dangerous situations.
  <span id="fb5"></span>
</div>

<button onclick="checkBlanks()">Check Answers</button>

<script>
  function checkBlanks() {
    const answers = [
      "hurricane",
      "shake",
      "flood",
      "warning",
      "protect"
    ];

    answers.forEach((correct, i) => {
      const input = document.getElementById("q" + (i + 1))
        .value.toLowerCase().trim();
      const feedback = document.getElementById("fb" + (i + 1));

      if (input === correct) {
        feedback.innerHTML = "✔ Correct";
        feedback.className = "correct";
      } else {
        feedback.innerHTML = "✘ Wrong";
        feedback.className = "wrong";
      }
    });
  }
</script>
```
